feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business One
feed.subtitle: SAP Business One Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: 480000112 - Batch/serial number 12345 selected in row 1 does not exist; specify a valid batch number
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to run a cURL command via cmd to post in service layer for a delivery note with a sales order as base document. The lines items are serialized. The cURL command is as follows:

{
"DocDate": "2026-03-09",
"DocDueDate": "2026-03-09",
"DocumentLines": [
{
"BaseType": 17,
"BaseEntry": 49,
"BaseLine": 0,
"Quantity": 1,
"SerialNumbers": [
{"InternalSerialNumber": "12345", "BaseLineNumber":0, "Quantity":1.0}
]
}
]
}

The error I am getting is as follows:

{
"error" : {
"code" : -5002,
"message" : {
"lang" : "en-us",
"value" : "480000112 - Batch/serial number 12345 selected in row 1 does not exist; specify a valid batch/serial number"
}
}
}

I appreciate your help.

Thank you,

Pascale

entry.title: SBO Mailer
entry.content:
Hello Friends,

                  In SAP B1 SBO mailer, how to disable or edit the standard table format? I check multiple ways, like changing the document summary layout and also checking any XML contained in the mailer file inside SAP's program files. But nothing will work. Please, help me. Is there any way to hide the table I attached the table picture in this chat. And also, is it possible to add a document's field data, like AR Invoices Customer ref number, in the SBO mailer.

entry.title: oInventoryGenEntry creation error
entry.content:
SAPbobsCOM.Recordset recordSet = (SAPbobsCOM.Recordset)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.BoRecordset);
recordSet.DoQuery($"SELECT TOP 1 DocEntry FROM OWOR WHERE Comments like '%{process.UserKey}%'");
if (!recordSet.EoF) // Ellenőrzés, hogy van-e eredmény
{

string docEntry = recordSet.Fields.Item("DocEntry").Value.ToString();

SAPbobsCOM.ProductionOrders productionOrder =
(SAPbobsCOM.ProductionOrders)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oProductionOrders);

int docEntryInt = int.Parse(docEntry);
bool found = productionOrder.GetByKey(docEntryInt);
Console.WriteLine($"DocEntry: {docEntryInt}, Found: {found}");
int productionOrderDocEntry = productionOrder.AbsoluteEntry;
if (process.Output == true)
{
recordSet.DoQuery($"SELECT TOP 1 DocEntry FROM OIGN WHERE Comments like '%{process.UserKey}%'");
if (recordSet.EoF)
{
SAPbobsCOM.Documents inventoryGenEntry = (SAPbobsCOM.Documents)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenEntry);
SAPbobsCOM.Items item = (SAPbobsCOM.Items)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oItems);
if (item.GetByKey(process.Material))
{
DateTime StartDate;
if (DateTime.TryParse(process.StartTime, out StartDate))
{
inventoryGenEntry.DocDate = StartDate;
}
inventoryGenEntry.Comments = "dSuite azonosító: " + process.UserKey + "\n" + process.Remarks;
inventoryGenEntry.DocObjectCode = SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenEntry;
//inventoryGenEntry.DocumentsOwner = 44;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseType = 202;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseEntry = productionOrderDocEntry;
//inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseLine = 0;

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Quantity = process.Quantity;
int result = inventoryGenEntry.Add();
if (result != 0)
{
Console.WriteLine($"Error: {company.GetLastErrorDescription()}");
Console.WriteLine($"Error: {company.GetLastErrorCode()}");
}
else
{
Console.WriteLine("Késztermék bevételezés sikeresen rögzítve a termelési rendeléshez!");
}
}
else
{
Console.WriteLine("Nem található cikk a jelentéshez");
}
}
else
{
SAPbobsCOM.Documents inventoryGenEntry = (SAPbobsCOM.Documents)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenEntry);
docEntry = recordSet.Fields.Item("DocEntry").Value.ToString();
docEntryInt = int.Parse(docEntry);
found = inventoryGenEntry.GetByKey(docEntryInt);
Console.WriteLine($"DocEntry InventoryGenEntry: {docEntryInt}, Found: {found}");
SAPbobsCOM.Items item = (SAPbobsCOM.Items)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oItems);
if (item.GetByKey(process.Material))
{
if (inventoryGenEntry.GetByKey(docEntryInt)) // A megfelelő sor alapján betöltjük a ProductionOrder objektumot
{
bool lineExists = false;
for (int i = 0; i < inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Count; i++)
{
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.SetCurrentLine(i);

if (inventoryGenEntry.Lines.ItemCode == process.Material.ToString())
{
lineExists = true;
break; // No need to continue checking
}
}

if (!lineExists)
{
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Quantity = process.Quantity; // Bevett mennyiség

// Kapcsolat a termelési rendeléshez
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseEntry = productionOrderDocEntry; // Termelési rendelés DocEntry
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseType =(int)SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oProductionOrders; // Alap dokumentum típusa

int result = inventoryGenEntry.Update();
if (result != 0)
{
Console.WriteLine($"Error: {company.GetLastErrorDescription()}");
}
else
{
Console.WriteLine("Késztermék bevételezés sikeresen rögzítve a termelési rendeléshez!");
}
}
}
}
}
}

The above is my code, and it always throws the same error, the employee is not exist or inactive.
"Error: (59001) [59/1/DocEntry/57294] * Nem létezo vagy inaktív dolgozóra nem lehet rögzíteni bizonylatot!
Error: -1116"
But I checked and there is an employee for my user which is loged in by the DI API, 
I also tried the InventoryGenEntry.DocumentOwner = 44 which is the userid in OUSR, and OHEM table too, but got the same error.

entry.title: SAP B1 log de modificaciones Revalorización de inventario
entry.content:
Tengo el caso en donde al momento de generar una revaluación de inventario no se visualiza dicho el cambio dentro del log de modificaciones del articulo como un registro adicional. 

El cambio al costo se aplica de forma correcta, pero en el log de modificaciones no se registra en la fecha en el que se realizó.

Saben si hay algo que se pueda hacer para que se visualice el registro de ese cambio?

Gracias de antemano

entry.title: How can I prevent certain users from executing reports in SBO V10 with Coresuite V8.3, while others
Anyone here who knows how to activate IFRS15 in SAP business one?
entry.content:
Customer needs to implement IFRS 15 in SAP business one. Anyone any know how to this?

Kind regards

Uta

entry.title: Internal Error - Inventory Transfer
entry.content:
Dear Team,

While adding an inventory transfer for a specific batch, the system is showing an error as Internal Error.

The following checks have already been performed:

  1. Transaction notification has been turned off from General Settings.
  2. All add-ons have been disconnected and tested.

Despite these checks, the issue persists.

entry.title: “Object reference not set to an instance of an object” when previewing Crystal Reports Layouts
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m facing an issue with SAP Business One related to Crystal Reports layouts.

When trying to preview any layout, I receive the following error:

Object reference not set to an instance of an object.

This issue occurs across all layouts and all databases on the main server where SAP B1 is installed.

However, I have a separate RDS server running SAP B1 Client where the same layouts work perfectly without any issues. So the problem seems specific to the main SAP server environment.

Additionally, when using the SAP tool:
“Preview External Crystal Reports File”, I get: Error while running Crystal report

Environment:

SAP B1 Version: SAP Business One 10.0 (10.00.300) FP 2508 (64-bit)

Crystal Reports Version: SAP Crystal Reports 14.3.4.4938

What I’ve tried so far:

  • Uninstalled and reinstalled Crystal Reports
  • Reinstalled CRRuntime
  • Verified that layouts preview correctly directly inside Crystal Reports (no issues there)

If anyone has encountered this issue or has suggestions, your help would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Receipt from Production: Unit Price Not Calculated from Actual Component Cost in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hello,

I am encountering a problem in SAP Business One during Receipt from Production.

Scenario:

  • I have a Production Order with actual component costs (Actual Item Component Cost).

  • When I post the Receipt from Production, the unit price of the finished product is not automatically calculated based on the Actual Item Component Cost.

  • This results in a variance between the component cost and the product cost.

Example:

  • Actual Item Component Cost = 1 000 000

  • Actual Product Cost = 1 200 000

  • Variance = 200 000

It seems that SAP is not automatically updating the product unit price from the actual component cost at the time of the receipt.

My questions:

  1. Is this expected behavior in SAP B1, or should the unit price always reflect the Actual Item Component Cost automatically?

  2. Is there a setting or configuration to ensure that the product unit price is calculated correctly from the actual component cost?

  3. Are there any best practices to avoid such variances or to handle this scenario correctly?

Thank you in advance for any guidance or insights.

entry.title: SAP Business one api gateway exporting the reports to excel
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have been testing the api gateway report service to download report as pdf with ExportPDFData.
However I need some reports as Excel, is there a method, to export a report to xlsx or csv formats? have this feature been asked before?

Additionaly when getting a report parameters with LoadCR, for any parameter with dynamic valu, no value are return. 
Is it possible to retrieve dynamic values for a parameter?

I'm using sap 10 FP 2502

Best Regards,

entry.title: SAP Business One on SAP CAL Version FP2508 or abrove
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I would like to ask about SAP Business One on SAP CAL.
Currently, I need a more up-to-date version (e.g., FP2508 or later). However, in SAP CAL, I can only find version FP2405.

Could you please advise on how I can access a newer version?
Is there any recommended approach or workaround to use FP2508 or above?

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: PLD Layout Issue – Page Footer Not Visible in Tax Invoice Preview.
entry.content:
In the PLD layout of the Tax Invoice, the page footer is not visible in the layout preview, even after adding it to the Page Footer section.

I have already tried removing and re-adding the footer, but it is still not appearing in the preview.

Could you please advise what settings or configurations need to be checked to make the page footer visible in the layout?

entry.title: InventoryCountingLine with UDF using DI API
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I have a strange problem to assign values to UDFs in InventoryCounting. Everything works fine except 2 UDFs in the countingline. Here some code:

SAPbobsCOM.Company oCompany = SwissAddonFramework.B1Connector.GetB1Connector().Company;
SAPbobsCOM.CompanyService oCS = (SAPbobsCOM.CompanyService) oCompany.GetCompanyService();

SAPbobsCOM.InventoryCountingsService oICS = oCS.GetBusinessService(SAPbobsCOM.ServiceTypes.InventoryCountingsService) as SAPbobsCOM.InventoryCountingsService;
SAPbobsCOM.InventoryCounting oIC = oICS.GetDataInterface(SAPbobsCOM.InventoryCountingsServiceDataInterfaces.icsInventoryCounting) as SAPbobsCOM.InventoryCounting;
SAPbobsCOM.InventoryCountingLines oICLS = oIC.InventoryCountingLines;

Grid myGrid = Grid.GetFromUID(pVal.Form, "grid_BSZB");
	
for (int i = 0; i < myGrid.Rows.Count; i++)
{
   SAPbobsCOM.InventoryCountingLine oICL = oICLS.Add();

   // some other code here to assign ItemCode etc.
   oICL.UserFields.Item("U_LAGPLATZ").Value = myGrid.GetValue("Lokation", i).ToString();
   oICL.UserFields.Item("U_FlagUpdate").Value = "Y";
}

SAPbobsCOM.InventoryCountingParams oICP = oICS.Add(oIC);

The document is saved without any error, but the UDFs keep empty. I checked of course with a messagebox, if the value from the grid is correct. Yes, it is. But not saved.

I restarted SAP but situation still the same. 

Is there anything else to do? We use SAP 10.0 (10.00.190) FP 2202. 

Thank you in advance for your help.

Fritz

entry.title: Tabela de vínculo de número de série em esboço (Draft) de NF de Saída no SAP Business One
entry.content:
Olá pessoal,

Preciso identificar em qual tabela é realizado o vínculo entre item da linha, e número de série em esboço de NF de Saída.

Quando selecionamos o número de série, e adicionamos a NF de Saída como esboço, ao fechar a tela e voltar no esboço, o número de série segue atribuído ao item, o que significa que isso fica registrado em alguma tabela.
Qual seria?

 

 

Encontrei apenas o vínculo para itens controlados por lote, que é por meio da tabela (DRF16 -> OBTN), quando estamos falando de esboço especificamente.
Para números de série não encontro.

Alguém poderia me ajudar? Por favor

Atte,

Gabriel Camargo

SAP Business One

entry.title: CALLING SAP HANA STORED PROCEDURES WITH VARIABLES
entry.content:
Community

I need to create a stored procedure that will be called from the query manager. I need to include variables so the user can input the values ​​they need, ideally dates and a subtype of an item. If I call the procedure with predefined values, it works, but I need it to be dynamic and allow the user to input that data.

If you could help me with how to structure this or how to call those variables from the query manager ([%0]), I would appreciate it.

entry.title: How to Control Grid Collapse/Expand Behavior to Show Only Item-Level Details in SAP Business One (UI
entry.content:
I am working on a custom grid in SAP Business One using a query (with UNION ALL) and facing an issue with the collapse/expand behavior.

Currently, the grid is grouped by DraftKey, and when I click to expand a row, it expands all columns, including:

  • Document Type
  • BP Name
  • BP Code
  • Item Code
  • Item Description
  • Document No
  • Originator
  • etc.

However, this is not the behavior I want.

Requirement:

I want the grid to behave like the Inventory Audit Report, where:

  • Header-level fields (such as DraftKey, Document Type, BP Name, BP Code, Document No, Originator, etc.) appear only once at the header level
  • On expanding a row (DraftKey), only item-level details should be shown, specifically:
    • Item Code
    • Item Description

In other words, I want a clear header-detail hierarchy, where:

  • Header = document-level data
  • Child rows = only item-level data

Current Situation:

  • I am using a SQL query with UNION ALL to structure the data
  • But due to this, all columns are repeating in expanded rows instead of separating into header and child levels

Reference Behavior:

I am attaching a screenshot of the Inventory Audit Report for reference, which demonstrates the exact expand/collapse behavior I want.
Inventory audit report

My Output:

I will also attach:

  • Screenshot of my current grid output
  • Relevant query/code snippet

 

 

 

 

Here is the source code: 

private void OpenResultForm()
{
try
{
string sql = BuildQuery();

string formUID = "APR_RESULT_" + DateTime.Now.Ticks;

SAPbouiCOM.FormCreationParams fcp =
(SAPbouiCOM.FormCreationParams)
EventHandler.oApplication.CreateObject(
SAPbouiCOM.BoCreatableObjectType.cot_FormCreationParams);

fcp.UniqueID = formUID;
fcp.FormType = "APR_RESULT";
fcp.BorderStyle = SAPbouiCOM.BoFormBorderStyle.fbs_Sizable;

SAPbouiCOM.Form oForm = EventHandler.oApplication.Forms.AddEx(fcp);

oForm.Title = "Cutomizable Approval Decision Report";
oForm.Width = 1200;
oForm.Height = 600;

SAPbouiCOM.Grid oGrid = null; 
oForm.Freeze(true);

try
{

// 1️ Add DataTable
oForm.DataSources.DataTables.Add("DT_1");


// 2 Add GRID 
SAPbouiCOM.Item oItem = oForm.Items.Add("GRID", SAPbouiCOM.BoFormItemTypes.it_GRID);

oItem.Left = 10;
oItem.Top = 10;
oItem.Width = oForm.Width - 40;
oItem.Height = oForm.Height - 120;


oGrid = (SAPbouiCOM.Grid)oItem.Specific;

// 3 Bind Grid to DataTable 
oGrid.DataTable = oForm.DataSources.DataTables.Item("DT_1");

// 4 Execute Query 
oForm.DataSources.DataTables.Item("DT_1").ExecuteQuery(sql);

oGrid.CollapseLevel = 1;


 private string BuildQuery()
{
string baseWhere = " WHERE 1 = 1 ";

string sql = $@"

SELECT
T0.""DraftEntry"" AS ""Draft Key"",
CASE T0.""ObjType""
WHEN '13' THEN 'A/R Invoice'
WHEN '14' THEN 'A/R Credit Memo'
WHEN '15' THEN 'Delivery'
WHEN '16' THEN 'Returns'
WHEN '17' THEN 'Sales Order'
WHEN '18' THEN 'A/P Invoice'
WHEN '19' THEN 'A/P Credit Memo'
WHEN '20' THEN 'Goods Receipt PO'
WHEN '21' THEN 'Goods Return'
WHEN '22' THEN 'Purchase Order'
WHEN '23' THEN 'Sales Quotation'
WHEN '67' THEN 'Inventory Transfer'
WHEN '46' THEN 'Outgoing Payments'
WHEN '24' THEN 'Incoming Payment'
WHEN '112' THEN 'Draft'
WHEN '1470000113' THEN 'Purchase Request'
ELSE T0.""ObjType""
END AS ""Document Type"",
T2.""CardName"" AS ""BP Name"",
T2.""CardCode"" AS ""BP Code"",
NULL AS ""Item Code"",
NULL AS ""Item Description"",
NULL AS ""Project"",
CASE T0.""ObjType""
WHEN '13' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""OINV"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
WHEN '14' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""ORIN"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
WHEN '15' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""ODLN"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
WHEN '16' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""ORDN"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
WHEN '17' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""ORDR"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
WHEN '18' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""OPCH"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
WHEN '19' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""ORPC"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
WHEN '20' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""OPDN"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
WHEN '21' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""ORPD"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
WHEN '22' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""OPOR"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
WHEN '23' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""OQUT"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
WHEN '46' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""OVPM"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
WHEN '24' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""ORCT"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
WHEN '67' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""OWTR"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
WHEN '1470000113' THEN (SELECT ""DocNum"" FROM ""OPRQ"" WHERE ""DocEntry"" = T0.""DocEntry"")
ELSE NULL
END AS ""Document No"",
T3.""U_NAME"" AS ""Originator"",
CASE T0.""ProcesStat""
WHEN 'W' THEN 'Pending'
WHEN 'P' THEN 'Generated'
WHEN 'A' THEN 'Generated by Authorizer'
WHEN 'C' THEN 'Canceled'
WHEN 'Y' THEN 'Approved'
WHEN 'N' THEN 'Rejected'
ELSE 'Unknown'
END AS ""Status"",
T0.""CreateDate"" AS ""Production Date"",
T1.""UserID"" AS ""UserID"",
T4.""WstCode"" AS ""StageCode"",
T4.""Name"" AS ""StageName"",
T0.""WddCode"" AS ""WddCode"",
T5.""U_NAME"" AS ""Authorizer"",
CASE T1.""Status""
WHEN 'W' THEN 'Pending'
WHEN 'Y' THEN 'Approved'
WHEN 'N' THEN 'Rejected'
ELSE 'Unknown'
END AS ""Answer"",
T1.""Substt"" AS ""Substitute Authorizer"",
T1.""Remarks"" AS ""Remarks"",
T2.""DocDueDate"" AS ""Due Date"",
T2.""DocDate"" AS ""Posting Date"",
T2.""DocNum"" AS ""Document Draft No"",
T1.""AuthUpdDat"" AS ""Draft Update Date"",
T1.""AuthUpdTim"" AS ""Draft Update Time"",
T2.""Comments"" AS ""Draft Remarks"",
T2.""DocTotal"" AS ""Doc Total (LC)"",
0 AS ""SortLevel""
FROM ""OWDD"" T0
INNER JOIN ""WDD1"" T1 ON T0.""WddCode"" = T1.""WddCode""
INNER JOIN ""ODRF"" T2 ON T0.""DraftEntry"" = T2.""DocEntry""
INNER JOIN ""OUSR"" T3 ON T0.""OwnerID"" = T3.""USERID""
INNER JOIN ""OUSR"" T5 ON T1.""UserID"" = T5.""USERID""
INNER JOIN ""OWST"" T4 ON T0.""CurrStep"" = T4.""WstCode""

{baseWhere}

UNION ALL

-- =========================
-- CHILD ROW (ITEMS)
-- =========================
SELECT
T0.""DraftEntry"" AS ""Draft Key"",
NULL AS ""Document Type"",
NULL AS ""BP Name"",
NULL AS ""BP Code"",
T6.""ItemCode"",
T6.""Dscription"",
T6.""Project"",
NULL AS ""Document No"",
NULL AS ""Originator"",
NULL AS ""Status"",
NULL AS ""Production Date"",
NULL AS ""UserID"",
NULL AS ""StageCode"",
NULL AS ""StageName"",
NULL AS ""WddCode"",
NULL AS ""Authorizer"",
NULL AS ""Answer"",
NULL AS ""Substitute Authorizer"",
NULL AS ""Remarks"",
NULL AS ""Due Date"",
NULL AS ""Posting Date"",
NULL AS ""Document Draft No"",
NULL AS ""Draft Update Date"",
NULL AS ""Draft Update Time"",
NULL AS ""Draft Remarks"",
NULL AS ""Doc Total (LC)"",
1 AS ""SortLevel""
FROM ""OWDD"" T0
INNER JOIN ""WDD1"" T1 ON T0.""WddCode"" = T1.""WddCode""
INNER JOIN ""ODRF"" T2 ON T0.""DraftEntry"" = T2.""DocEntry""
INNER JOIN ""DRF1"" T6 ON T2.""DocEntry"" = T6.""DocEntry""
INNER JOIN ""OUSR"" T3 ON T0.""OwnerID"" = T3.""USERID""
INNER JOIN ""OUSR"" T5 ON T1.""UserID"" = T5.""USERID""
INNER JOIN ""OWST"" T4 ON T0.""CurrStep"" = T4.""WstCode""


{baseWhere}

ORDER BY
""Draft Key"",
""SortLevel"" ASC";
}

Question:

How can I achieve this type of controlled expand/collapse behavior in SAP Business One grid?

  • Is it possible to achieve this header-detail expand behavior using only SQL (UNION ALL + sorting)?
  • Or is this behavior internally handled by SAP (like Inventory Audit Report) and not reproducible via Grid?
  • If this requires UI API handling, what is the correct approach?
    • Should I use Matrix instead of Grid?
    • Or is there a way to control column visibility per collapse level in Grid?

Key Concern:

I want to avoid repeating header fields in child rows and display only relevant item-level columns when expanded.

Any guidance, best practices, or sample implementations would be really helpful.

entry.title: non claimable tax code does not show in Tax Report - Tax Register book
entry.content:
Hi All,
A user is running  the Tax Report - Tax Register Book - with all tax codes selected.

This particular invoice has 4 lines

But only the line with the tax amount shows in the report even though all tax codes are selected.

Any reason why this is? It is standard behaviour of how the report is generated. 

Thank you
Jerusha

entry.title: Accessing Practice System for SAP Business One 10.0 (HANA) - Managing Logistics Course
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently working through the "Managing Logistics in SAP Business One" course as part of the SAP Certified - SAP Business One certification path.

I’ve reached the "Case Study: Introduction SAP Business One 10.0" section, which asks to use the demo database. While I have the PDF instructions and the login credentials, I cannot find the actual link or "Enter System" button to launch the cloud-based practice environment on the SAP Learning portal.

Could someone guide me to where the practice system enrollment is located for this specific course? Is there a prerequisite step I might be missing to make the "Practice" tab visible?

Thanks in advance for your help!

SAP Business One, version for SAP HANA 

, I cannot find the

entry.title: How an I traverse thru tables and fill a result table?
entry.content:
BusinessOne objects in SAP rest in a bunch of tables, that have a numeric ID but a text name - for whatever reason.
The first function just gets me the correct table:

CREATE FUNCTION "q_GetDocLinesTable"
/**
 * Get B1 objects table of a documents lines
 * V1.0 Coded 2026-02-05 by .rhavin;)
 */
(
	/* B1 Document Type */
	IN ObjType int
)
	/* B1 Document Meta Table */
	RETURNS TableName NVARCHAR(10)

	LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT 

AS

BEGIN

	IF     (:ObjType=13) THEN TableName := 'INV1'; /* A/R Invoice */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=14) THEN TableName := 'RIN1'; /* A/R Credit Memo */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=15) THEN TableName := 'DLN1'; /* Delivery */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=16) THEN TableName := 'RDN1'; /* Returns */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=17) THEN TableName := 'RDR1'; /* Sales Order */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=18) THEN TableName := 'PCH1'; /* A/P Invoice */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=19) THEN TableName := 'RPC1'; /* A/P Credit Memo */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=20) THEN TableName := 'PDN1'; /* Goods Receipt PO */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=21) THEN TableName := 'RPD1'; /* Goods Return */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=22) THEN TableName := 'POR1'; /* Purchase Order */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=23) THEN TableName := 'QUT1'; /* Sales Quotation */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=24) THEN TableName := 'RCT1'; /* Incoming Payment */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=67) THEN TableName := 'WTR1'; /* Incoming Payment */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=540000006) THEN TableName := 'PQT1'; /* WTF? */
	ELSE TableName := NULL;
	END IF;
End;

I also created a type that I need to fill:

CREATE TYPE "q_tt_EntryMap" AS TABLE (
	"Entry" INTEGER,
	"Type" INTEGER
);

Now, what I want to is:

  • Step 1: Start with any given combination of Entry/Type
  • Step 2: fill a resulting table of type q_tt_EntryMap with the combination (if found)
  • Step 3: if any "BaseEntry": move to that combination and goto Step 2.

The following function gets me the first entry, but I'm unsure how to append to my result-table and how to move on to the next entry:

CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE "q_GetEntryTable"
(
EntryID int,
EntryType int
)

LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT 
SQL SECURITY INVOKER

AS
	LineTable     NVARCHAR(10);
	tSQL          NVARCHAR(4000);
	Result        "q_tt_EntryMap";

BEGIN

	LineTable := "q_GetDocLinesTable"(EntryType);
--	WHILE (:LineTable IS NOT NULL)
--	BEGIN
		tSQL := '
		SELECT DISTINCT "BaseEntry" AS "Entry", "BaseType" AS "Type"
		FROM ' || :LineTable || '
		WHERE "DocEntry" = ' || :EntryID || ' AND "BaseType" <> -1
		';
		EXECUTE IMMEDIATE tSQL into Result; /* ## How do I append? ## */
--		LineTable := "q_GetDocLinesTable"(/* ## Somehow put Type-Field of last row of :result into it ## */);


--	END;
		
	SELECT * FROM :Result;

END;

 

So if [1234,15] has a BaseEntry of [2345,13] and that has a BaseEntry of [3456,17] (I just made up those numbers), the resulting table returned should look like this:

(Entry)(Type)
123415
234513
345617

Of course, if there is a better way to do this, please feel free to tell me.

entry.title: SAP B1 10.00.300 FP 2508 - The solution package does not have the right format or does not exist
entry.content:
Hello,

We are having SAP Business One version 10.00.300 FP 2508 and MS SQL 2022 and we are facing the following issue.

When we are trying to create a new company from solution package, It has error "The solution package does not have the right format or does not exist".

Please help me. Thank you very much
